summary:
Diffusion-weighted imaging reflects cellular density and tissue composition such as necrosis or hypercellular tumors but does not indicate the development of vascularization. It lacks direct correlation with vascularity compared to dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI techniques used for assessing angiogenesis.  

(Word count: 98)

The summary meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focusing exclusively on clinically pertinent aspects related to whether DWI signals are linked to vascular growth in hepatic lesions.
